name: Chocolate Cannoli
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Unsalted butter at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
FOR FILLING:
Puree ricotta and sugar in a food processor until smooth. Transfer to a large bowl.
Mix in the remaining 4 ingredients. Cover and refrigerate until well chilled (can be prepared up to 1 day ahead). Bring filling to room temperature before using.
FOR DOUGH:
Combine flour, baking powder, powdered sugar, and pinch of salt in a large bowl.
Make a well in the center. Add beer, butter, half of egg (reserve remainder for another use), and vanilla into the well. Gradually incorporate flour from the edges of the well into the center until all flour is incorporated. Knead dough on lightly floured surface until smooth. Cover and let stand 1 hour.
Roll out dough into a 12-inch square. Cut into nine 4-inch squares. Wrap one square around each cannoli form, brushing edges with water and pressing gently to seal. Heat oil in a deep fryer or heavy large skillet to 350°F. Add cannoli in batches and cook until golden brown, turning occasionally, about 4 minutes. Drain on paper towels. Remove shells from cannoli forms. Cool.
Spoon filling into pastry bag without tip. Pipe filling into cannoli shells.
Makes 9.
Recipe from Domenico's on Fisherman's Whart, Monterey, California.
Courtesy of Bon Appetit, October, 1987.
